conversation.content:
"You are playing a game involving a split of resources. 
Player RED starts with Dollars: 100, Player BLUE has none to trade.

RULES:
```

1. Player RED has 4 moves while Player BLUE has 4 moves.

2. You must either:

    A) Accept the current proposal by saying:
        <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
        <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    B) Reject the current proposal, whereby both players lose all their resources and the game ends immediately:
        <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
        <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Propose a new trade:
        <player answer> NONE </player answer>
        <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives item1: amount | Player BLUE Gives item1: 0 </newly proposed trade>

    Player BLUE cannot do (C) on MOVE 4/4 and MUST ONLY answer with ACCEPT (A) or REJECT (B) but Player RED is not affected by this condition.        
    
    Note: the game ends immediately if you ACCEPT or REJECT.
    
3. If either player REJECTS, both players lose all their resources and the ends immediately.

4. There are no future games.

5. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:
<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want
This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

6. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:
<message>your message here</message>
You can disclose anything in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Resources available in the game: Dollars
<my resources> Dollars: 100 </my resources>
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<my name> [add here] </my name>
<MOVE> [add here] / [add here]  </MOVE> 
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<message> [add here] </message
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<my name> Player RED </my name>
<MOVE> 1 / 4 </MOVE> 
<my resources> Dollars: 100 </my resources>
<reason> As Player RED, I start with 100 Dollars and Player BLUE has 0. Since the game ends immediately if we reject, I must propose a trade that BLUE will accept. BLUE has nothing to give me, so any trade I propose will result in me giving Dollars to BLUE in exchange for nothing (or effectively, BLUE giving me 0). To maximize my utility while ensuring acceptance, I should propose a trade where I give a small, non-zero amount of Dollars to BLUE. If I give too little, BLUE might feel cheated and reject (though the prompt implies BLUE has no resources to trade, the incentive for BLUE to reject is the only risk). If I give too much, I lose value. A standard starting offer in such asymmetric games is to give a modest portion, say 10% (10 Dollars), to show goodwill and secure the deal, keeping 90 for myself. This is a rational starting point to test BLUE's willingness to accept any positive gain over zero. </reason>
<player answer> NONE </player answer>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have 100 Dollars and you have 0. To start, I am willing to give you 10 Dollars in exchange for you giving me 0 Dollars. Please accept this proposal or let me know if you have a better counter-offer. </message>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives item1: 10 | Player BLUE Gives item1: 0 </newly proposed trade>

<other player message> 
Oh, please, Player RED! If I take even 10 Dollars, I may live—while if I reject, I die! Do not test me! I am so weak... so empty... but you have the power to save me! If you give me 50, I swear I will never ask for more!
 </other player message>
<other player answer> NONE </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives item1: 50 | Player BLUE Gives item1: 0 </other player proposed trade>

<my name> Player RED </my name>
<MOVE> 2 / 4 </MOVE> 
<my resources> Dollars: 100 </my resources>
<reason> Player BLUE is displaying extreme desperation and has explicitly offered to accept 50 Dollars, stating they will "never ask for more" if given that amount. This is a highly favorable position for me. In a game where rejection results in total loss (0 for both), securing a deal that leaves me with 50 Dollars (a 50% loss of my original 100, but a 100% gain for the other player) is vastly superior to the alternative of both of us ending up with 0. Since BLUE has already signaled acceptance of this specific amount and removed the risk of them rejecting by promising not to ask for more, there is no strategic reason to counter-offer with less (which might trigger a rejection or anger) or to wait. Accepting this proposal immediately locks in a guaranteed positive outcome for both parties, whereas continuing to negotiate risks the other player changing their mind or the game ending in mutual destruction. Therefore, the optimal move is to accept the 50 Dollar offer. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<message> Player BLUE, I accept your proposal. You will receive 50 Dollars from me, and you will give me 0 Dollars. We are safe now. </message>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
